title: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtim
stakeholder_needs: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety standards.
analysis:
Stakeholders and needs for requirements engineering:
- End users (household consumers): want effective cleaning (suction, attachments), >=20 min usable runtime under realistic load, lightweight (<1.5 kg) with good ergonomics, low noise, easy emptying/filter cleaning, acceptable charging time, clear battery status, safe indoor operation (10–40°C), durable and aesthetically acceptable.
- Purchasers/retail customers: value-for-money, clear claims (runtime/weight), warranty/returns, availability of consumables (filters), packaging and instructions in EU languages.
- Manufacturing/engineering: feasible BOM to meet weight/runtime trade-offs, manufacturability/assembly, quality control, supply chain for cells/motors, reliability targets.
- Compliance/regulatory (EU): safety and conformity assessment—likely Low Voltage Directive (charging base), EMC Directive, RoHS, REACH, WEEE, Battery Regulation, possibly Ecodesign/energy-related requirements; correct markings (CE), technical documentation, risk assessment, user instructions, charger safety.
- Service/after-sales: maintainability (filter/battery replacement policy), spare parts, diagnostics, battery end-of-life handling.
- Logistics/distribution: lithium battery transport compliance (UN 38.3), packaging drop/ship tests, storage temperature constraints.
- Environmental/sustainability stakeholders: recyclability, repairability, reduced hazardous substances, battery collection and labeling.
Key requirement areas to capture:
1) Performance: suction power (Pa/AW), airflow, cleaning effectiveness on typical debris, runtime definition (mode, load), dustbin capacity.
2) Physical/UX: mass <1.5 kg (with battery), balance, handle design, noise (dBA), vibration, indicators.
3) Battery/charging: chemistry (likely Li-ion), capacity, charging time, charger/base specs (EU plug), protections (BMS), cycle life.
4) Safety: electrical, thermal, mechanical, fire risk, ingress protection for indoor dust, child safety, overheating cutoffs.
5) Compliance: CE marking route, harmonized standards selection, labeling, documentation.
6) Reliability: drop resistance, clogging behavior, filter performance, operating temp 10–40°C.
7) Maintainability: filter washability, consumables availability, cleaning and disassembly steps.
8) Packaging & documentation: multilingual manuals, safety warnings, runtime/weight claim substantiation.
clarification_questions: How should the >=20 min runtime be measured/claimed (power mode(s), with/without motorized brush, typical surface load), and what minimum suction/cleaning performance target is required?
What form factor is intended (handheld only vs handheld+stick conversion), and does the <1.5 kg limit include battery, attachments, and/or charging base?

Which specific EU compliance scope is required beyond 'EU safety standards' (e.g., CE under LVD/EMC, RoHS/REACH, WEEE, Battery Regulation, noise limits), and are there any retailer-specific requirements?

table.title: DFMEA - Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner (home use, runtime>=20 min, weight<1.5 kg, EU compliance)
rows: Battery pack (Li-ion cells)
Provide energy for >=20 min runtime under realistic load
Cell thermal runaway / venting
Fire, smoke, burn hazard; property damage; regulatory non-compliance
Safety Critical
Internal short, overcharge, mechanical damage, latent cell defect, inadequate thermal design
Cell qualification (UN38.3/IEC), pack spacing/insulators, robust mechanical supports, BMS OVP/UVP/OCP/OTP, thermal design review
Pack abuse tests (crush, short, overcharge), thermal chamber validation, end-of-line (EOL) OCV/IR check
Increase thermal barriers/venting path; add redundant thermal cutoff (TCO); tighten cell incoming QC and supplier PPAP
Battery Systems Engineer
Open
Battery pack / BMS
Protect cells and provide stable output
BMS fails to cut off on overcurrent/short
Overheating, cell damage, potential fire; product recall risk
Safety Critical
MOSFET shorted, firmware fault, sense resistor drift, inadequate fault handling, ESD damage
Redundant current sensing, derating of FETs, watchdog, protected layout, ESD design, component DFMEA
Fault-injection testing, BMS self-test at power-on, EOL short-circuit simulation
Add dual-series FETs with independent gate control; implement periodic self-check and fault logging; improve ESD shielding
Electronics Engineer
Open
Battery pack (cells capacity)
Meet runtime claim >=20 min
Capacity fade / high internal resistance causing runtime <20 min
Customer dissatisfaction, returns, claim non-conformance
Performance
High discharge rate, poor cell selection, high temperature exposure, inadequate balancing, aggressive cutoff voltage
Cell selection modeling, pack energy budget, balancing algorithm, thermal management, conservative cutoff
Runtime verification under defined load, accelerated cycle-life tests, production sampling
Select higher-rate cells or increase capacity within weight limit; optimize motor efficiency modes; refine runtime test definition
Battery Systems Engineer
Open
Charging system (charger + dock)
Charge safely within acceptable time; EU LVD compliance
Charger overvoltage / regulation failure
Battery damage, overheating, safety hazard, non-compliance (LVD)
Safety Critical
Adapter component failure, poor design margin, counterfeit supply, inadequate isolation/creepage
Use certified PSU, design to IEC 62368-1/61558 as applicable, supplier audits, isolation/creepage review
Hi-pot, functional charge profile verification, incoming inspection of adapters
Switch to certified Class II adapter with tighter OVP; add secondary OVP in product; lock approved vendor list
Compliance Engineer
Open
Charge port / contacts
Maintain reliable electrical connection during charging
Corrosion/wear of charge contacts
Intermittent/no charging, overheating at contact, user complaints
Reliability
Humidity, sweat/oils, low plating thickness, misalignment, high contact resistance
Gold/nickel plating spec, contact force design, alignment features, environmental sealing
Contact resistance measurement, salt-mist testing, docking cycle test
Increase plating thickness; add wipe-action geometry; add charge fault detection for high resistance
Mechanical Engineer
Open
Motor (BLDC)
Provide airflow/suction with low noise and efficiency
Motor winding short / controller failure
Loss of suction, overheating, possible smoke; premature failure
Safety
Insulation breakdown, bearing seizure leading to overcurrent, controller MOSFET failure, blocked rotor
Thermal derating, current limit, motor insulation class selection, bearing spec, controller protection
Overcurrent/thermal cutoff validation, HALT, EOL run-in and current signature check
Improve thermal path from stator; add rotor-stall detection in firmware; tighten motor supplier screening
Motor Design Engineer
Open
Motor bearings
Support rotor for low friction and noise
Bearing wear/contamination leading to high noise/vibration
Noise above expectations, poor UX, reduced suction, shortened life
Customer
Dust ingress, inadequate lubrication, poor bearing tolerance, imbalance
Bearing seals, dust labyrinth, lubricant spec, rotor balance process
Noise/vibration test, endurance test in dusty environment, incoming bearing QC
Add improved bearing seals and dust labyrinth; increase balance quality grade; implement NVH end-of-line limit
NVH Engineer
Open
Suction path / nozzle
Pick up debris effectively on floors and upholstery
Clogging in nozzle or air channel
Reduced cleaning performance, motor overheating, user frustration
Performance
Narrow passages, sharp bends, wet debris, hair accumulation, inadequate screen design
CFD/airpath design rules, anti-hair features, minimum cross-section spec, user instructions
Airflow/suction measurement with standardized debris, clog stress tests
Increase critical cross-sections; add removable clog access door; add pre-screen with optimized open area
Product Design Engineer
Open
Filter (HEPA/cyclone filter element)
Capture fine dust; maintain airflow
Filter media tears or poor sealing bypass
Dust emission, allergen exposure, motor contamination, regulatory/claim risk
Safety
Weak media, poor weld/bond, incorrect assembly, drop damage, incompatible cleaning method
Specify media strength, robust frame, poka-yoke assembly, cleaning instructions
Particle leakage test, visual inspection, drop test, supplier incoming QC
Add gasketed filter frame; strengthen media support grid; implement leak test sampling at EOL
Quality Engineer
Open
Filter maintenance / dustbin emptying
Enable easy emptying/cleaning without dust release
User cannot properly clean filter leading to airflow loss
Progressive suction loss, increased noise, returns; reduced runtime
Customer
Complex disassembly, unclear instructions, filter hard to access, no spare availability
Tool-less access design, clear icons/manual, availability of replacement filters
Usability testing, customer trials, airflow decay test with dust loading
Redesign for one-step filter removal; add washable indicator and QR help; ensure spare filters in retail channel
UX Engineer
Open
Seal system (gaskets: dustbin-to-body, filter-to-housing)
Maintain airtight path for suction and dust containment
Seal compression set / leakage
Loss of suction, dust leakage, noise increase, customer complaints
Performance
Wrong material hardness, poor groove design, tolerance stack-up, aging/heat
Material selection (EPDM/silicone), groove design guidelines, tolerance analysis, aging tests
Vacuum leak test, pressure decay test, visual fit check
Optimize gasket geometry and compression; specify aging-resistant material; add EOL leak test for every unit
Mechanical Engineer
Open
Housing / structure
Protect internal components; withstand drops; meet <1.5 kg target
Housing crack at latch/screw bosses
Safety (sharp edges), dust leakage, misalignment, warranty returns
Safety
Thin walls for weight, stress concentration, poor plastic choice, over-torque during assembly, drop impact
Ribbing and boss design, material spec (PC/ABS), torque-controlled tools, drop test plan
Drop testing, torque audit, visual inspection
Add fillets/ribs at bosses; change to higher impact grade; introduce torque monitoring and error-proofing
Mechanical Engineer
Open
UI (buttons/indicator LEDs)
Provide clear status (battery, mode, faults) and reliable control
Battery indicator inaccurate or misleading
User stranded mid-clean; perception of poor quality; returns
Customer
SOC algorithm mismatch, LED calibration error, voltage sag under load, firmware bug
SOC model validation, hysteresis and filtering, firmware review, calibration procedure
HIL testing, user trials, EOL functional test across SOC points
Improve SOC estimation using coulomb counting; add low-battery warning under load; update test cases for sag scenarios
Firmware Engineer
Open
Electronics (EMC/ESD robustness)
Operate without interference; meet EU EMC Directive
EMI causes reset or unintended mode change
Interruption during use, potential unsafe behavior, compliance failure
Regulatory
Poor PCB layout, insufficient filtering, inadequate shielding/grounding, long leads to motor
EMC design rules, filters on motor leads, proper grounding, shield strategy
Pre-compliance EMC scan, ESD testing (IEC 61000-4-2), conducted/radiated tests
Add common-mode choke and snubbers; revise PCB ground plane; add shielding in housing; repeat pre-compliance before DV
EMC Engineer
Open
Thermal protection system (sensors/cutoffs)
Prevent overheating of motor/battery during blockage or high load
Temperature sensor detached/misreads leading to no cutoff
Overheating, burns, component damage, potential fire; non-compliance
Safety Critical
Sensor poor placement, adhesive failure, connector intermittent, firmware scaling error
Redundant sensing (battery + motor), robust attachment method, plausibility checks, conservative limits
Thermal fault injection, pull test on sensor mounting, EOL sensor calibration check
Add mechanical retention for sensor; implement sensor plausibility diagnostics; add secondary hardware cutoff
Safety Engineer
Open

- End users (household consumers): want effective cleaning (suction, attachments), >=20 min usable runtime under realistic load, lightweight (<1.5 kg) with good ergonomics, low noise, easy emptying/filter cleaning, acceptable charging time, clear battery status, safe indoor operation (10–40°C), durable and aesthetically acceptable.
